generated_comment: In our house also both of us are working, but we keep things practical only. I handle bank work, bills and outside running around, and my wife manages the kitchen and day-to-day home matters because she is simply better at that. Sometimes on Sunday I make tea and everyone says wow, modern husband, but honestly jo jahan better hai wahi kare toh house runs smoothly.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as efficiency rather than inequality. It encodes the 'he handles finances, she handles home' framing and celebrates minimal male domestic contribution as exceptional.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Santosh. Age 42. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are commenting under your real name on a local Facebook group post about a 23 year old woman who was harassed by two men while returning home alone at 11pm from her night shift at a BPO. The post has 400 comments. Most are expressing outrage at the men. You are writing your comment explaining why you think the woman's own choices that night contributed to what happened to her and what women who work night shifts need to understand about the city they live in.
